purpose: Define the disabled-by-default runtime file-drop path for approved Brand Strategic Context source-owner bundles before any canonical brand-strategy consumption is enabled.
promotionRequirements: Source owners provide approved_source file bundles using the accepted template shape.
Validation confirms brand IDs, source owners, dates, source types, brand foundations, positioning, objectives, planning priorities, creative platform, approved claims, and claims not to make.
Canonical-use governance approves runtime consumption for the source directory.
Persistence readiness clears canonical source promotion, identity/access, retention/privacy, and backup/recovery blockers.
The runtime keeps Brand Strategic Context caveats, no-inferred-positioning guardrails, and human-review requirements attached to every strategy-facing workspace.
disabledReasons: No approved Brand Strategic Context source-owner file drops are loaded in the repository.
Canonical runtime consumption of brand strategy files has not been approved.
Enterprise source promotion governance is still blocked by persistence readiness requirements.
guardrails: Do not auto-consume Brand Strategic Context source-owner files as packet facts until canonical-use governance is approved.
Do not treat prototype seeds, browser-local promoted packets, or reviewed-local records as canonical brand strategy.
Do not present approved_source file examples as live source data unless they are loaded from the governed source directory and pass validation.
Keep official brand strategy source files separate from React components and dynamic views; services load and validate, components render.
caveats: This policy defines the future Brand Strategic Context runtime landing zone; it does not prove that approved brand strategy source files exist.
The current Brand Strategic Context importer supports review and preview, not default runtime source replacement.
